point_support.reason: COVID-19 is described as a contagious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
The document states that COVID-19 spread worldwide starting in January 2020 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
WHO declared COVID-19 a global health emergency in March 2020 and ended it in May 2023 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000].
COVID-19 is shorthand for 'coronavirus disease 2019', named by WHO on 11 February 2020 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00009].
COVID-19 can be diagnosed with RT-PCR or other nucleic acid tests and confirmed with chest CT scans [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00079].
COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2 [node_c:0:7f6104cc].
COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2, spreading in late 2019 and becoming a pandemic in 2020 [node_b:0:4dbd49a8].
COVID-19 can cause mild illness or no symptoms, but can lead to hospitalization or death in older adults and those with certain conditions [node_b:2:736f7311].
It's possible to get COVID-19 more than once due to fading immunity or virus mutations [node_b:3:eced3163].
Precautions to prevent COVID-19 include vaccination, physical distancing, mask wearing, and handwashing [node_c:4:f813ab80].

The document states that COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2 and became a pandemic in 2020.
The document explains that the virus spreads through the air via droplets from coughs, sneezes, or talking.
The document mentions that COVID-19 can cause mild illness or no symptoms, but can be severe in older adults and those with certain conditions.
The document notes that it's possible to get COVID-19 more than once due to fading immunity or virus mutations.
The document lists preventive measures such as vaccination, good airflow, physical distancing, mask-wearing, and hygiene.
External evidence confirms COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2.
External evidence provides the naming details of COVID-19 by WHO.
External evidence confirms COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2.
External evidence describes the diagnostic methods for COVID-19.
External evidence provides details on WHO's declaration of COVID-19 as a global health emergency.

The document confirms COVID-19 vaccines undergo rigorous testing for safety and effectiveness.

External evidence supports that COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2.
